purpose: Map the smallest repository surfaces a Pi-backed agent needs for intake, bounded orchestration, validation, artifact production, and handoff.

directories.purpose: Canonical manifests, schemas, workflows, registries, fixtures, and proof policy.
Scoped agent procedures; no product behavior belongs only in prose.
Pi adapter maps, workflows, schemas, and artifact contracts.
Opt-in local validation hooks; never installed implicitly.
Repository-level deterministic validators and read-only probes.
English operator guidance, status, gaps, risks, and proof ceilings.

knownTraps: Do not infer Pi installation, provider readiness, privacy, or model delivery from configuration files.
Do not execute third-party extension snippets until the pinned upstream API and permissions are reviewed.
Enforce one writer per branch; architect, builder, validator, and adjudicator roles must not share write authority.
Do not fuse unattributed outputs or hide unresolved divergence.
Do not run autovalidation without attempt, wall-clock, token, cancellation, and no-progress bounds.
Do not store prompts, raw model output, credentials, or local endpoint evidence in tracked artifacts.
proofCeiling: Repository map and deterministic offline contracts only; no Pi runtime, provider, model, extension, network, privacy, or code-delivery proof.
